title: Listening - Part 2
description: For each question, choose the correct answer.
instructions: You will hear six short extracts. For questions 8–13, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best according to what you hear.

extracts.context: You will hear two people talking about buying a bicycle.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: Have you found a bicycle yet? || Man: Not really. I went to that shop near the station, but the assistant only showed me the most expensive models. I thought about looking online, but there are hundreds of choices. || Woman: Online reviews can be useful, but they may not answer your questions. [8]Why don't you speak to my uncle? He repairs bikes for a living and knows exactly what beginners need[/8]. || Man: That's a good idea. I'll ask him before I buy anything.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Have you found a bicycle yet?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Not really. I went to that shop near the station, but the assistant only showed me the most expensive models. I thought about looking online, but there are hundreds of choices.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Online reviews can be useful, but they may not answer your questions. Why don't you speak to my uncle? He repairs bikes for a living and knows exactly what beginners need.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: That's a good idea. I'll ask him before I buy anything.

questions.question: The woman suggests that the man should
questions.explanation: The woman suggests speaking to her uncle, who repairs bikes, so she thinks the man should get expert advice.

extracts.context: You will hear a man telling his friend about his language course.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: How's your evening Spanish course going? || Man: Better than I expected, actually. I was worried the grammar would be impossible, but we're doing it slowly. The lessons are only an hour, which suits me after work. [9]The only problem is the teacher's speed. When she explains something new, she talks so quickly that I sometimes miss the important parts[/9]. || Woman: Could you ask her to slow down? || Man: I will. Everyone else seems to feel the same.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: How's your evening Spanish course going?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Better than I expected, actually. I was worried the grammar would be impossible, but we're doing it slowly. The lessons are only an hour, which suits me after work. The only problem is the teacher's speed. When she explains something new, she talks so quickly that I sometimes miss the important parts.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Could you ask her to slow down?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I will. Everyone else seems to feel the same.

questions.question: What problem does the man mention?
questions.explanation: The man says the teacher talks so quickly that he misses important parts.

extracts.context: You will hear a woman telling a colleague about her weekend.
extracts.audio_script: Man: Did you do anything exciting at the weekend? || Woman: We planned to visit a big exhibition in town, but my daughter wasn't feeling well, so we stayed at home. At first I was disappointed because I wanted to go somewhere new. In the end, though, [10]it was lovely just to sit in the garden with a book while the children played quietly nearby[/10]. || Man: That sounds peaceful. || Woman: It was. I don't get many weekends like that.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Did you do anything exciting at the weekend?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: We planned to visit a big exhibition in town, but my daughter wasn't feeling well, so we stayed at home. At first I was disappointed because I wanted to go somewhere new. In the end, though, it was lovely just to sit in the garden with a book while the children played quietly nearby.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: That sounds peaceful.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: It was. I don't get many weekends like that.

questions.question: What did the woman like about her weekend?

options.A: visiting a new place in the city

questions.explanation: She says it was lovely to sit in the garden with a book, which shows she liked relaxing.

extracts.context: You will hear two friends talking about a new restaurant.
extracts.audio_script: Man: Have you tried the new Italian restaurant yet? || Woman: Yes, last Friday. The pasta tasted fresh, and I thought the prices were fair. || Man: I went on Saturday. My pizza was great, but we waited almost forty minutes before anyone took our order. || Woman: Exactly. [11]The food is good enough, but they really need to bring meals to the tables more quickly[/11]. || Man: I agree. I don't mind paying if I don't have to spend the whole evening waiting.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Have you tried the new Italian restaurant yet?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Yes, last Friday. The pasta tasted fresh, and I thought the prices were fair.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I went on Saturday. My pizza was great, but we waited almost forty minutes before anyone took our order.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Exactly. The food is good enough, but they really need to bring meals to the tables more quickly.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I agree. I don't mind paying if I don't have to spend the whole evening waiting.

questions.question: They both think the restaurant would be better if
questions.explanation: Both speakers say the food and prices are fine, but the service needs to be quicker.

extracts.context: You will hear two old friends talking at a party.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: Mark? I nearly didn't recognise you! || Man: Really? I haven't changed that much, have I? || Woman: Well, you still have the same smile. I thought you'd grown a beard from one of your holiday photos, but it's gone now. || Man: Yes, I shaved it last month. || Woman: [12]It's the jacket and smart shoes that surprised me. You always used to wear old jeans and trainers[/12]. || Man: I have to look serious for work these days.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Mark? I nearly didn't recognise you!

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Really? I haven't changed that much, have I?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Well, you still have the same smile. I thought you'd grown a beard from one of your holiday photos, but it's gone now.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Yes, I shaved it last month.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: It's the jacket and smart shoes that surprised me. You always used to wear old jeans and trainers.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I have to look serious for work these days.

questions.question: How is the man's appearance different from before?

options.C: He has changed his style of clothes.

questions.explanation: The woman says his jacket and smart shoes are different from the old jeans and trainers he used to wear.

extracts.context: You will hear two colleagues talking about a meeting.
extracts.audio_script: Man: Are you ready for the planning meeting this afternoon? || Woman: I thought it had been cancelled. Isn't the manager away today? || Man: She is, but she's joining us online. || Woman: Oh, really? [13]I'm surprised we're still having it without her in the office[/13]. I don't mind attending, and my notes are ready, but I expected it to move to next week. || Man: No, she wants the decision made today. || Woman: That sounds sensible. || Man: Yes, I think it will make things much easier next time.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Are you ready for the planning meeting this afternoon?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I thought it had been cancelled. Isn't the manager away today?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: She is, but she's joining us online.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Oh, really? I'm surprised we're still having it without her in the office. I don't mind attending, and my notes are ready, but I expected it to move to next week.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: No, she wants the decision made today.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: That sounds sensible.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Yes, I think it will make things much easier next time.

questions.question: How does the woman feel about the meeting?

options.A: surprised that it is still going to take place
options.B: annoyed that she will have to attend it
options.C: worried that her notes are not ready

questions.explanation: The woman says she is surprised the meeting is still happening while the manager is away.
